posts.title: Fail Safe Logic
posts.author: Admin
posts.excerpt: Fail Safe Logic in each project often not goes to advance level.
posts.content:
Introduction

In almost all the project, fail safe design and logic is given less importance and carried out only for customer satisfaction. Any Fail Safe logic must have details like which Safety Category has been adopted, how it is designed and how it is going to be executed. Customer will be more curious and will realize how serious are we about safety. Wiring E-Stop to safety relay or PLC often goes with theory logic. This must be analyzed in detail, designer need to adopt a system to understand what type of safety ,customer requires or if he is not aware, based on project 3D CAD needs to present safety category adopted in design.

posts.title: Push_Button and Indicators Requirement Form
posts.author: Admin
posts.excerpt: Fill this form and get approved from customer and share the file with Electrical and Mechanical Designer.
posts.content:
Ctrl+K Ctrl+J Push Button and Lamps.xlsx Read the document carefully and create me a form that can be kept to understand how many pushbutton, indicators and lamps are required? what should be the color of those? which color tower lamp should glow for Machine Idle, Machine Running, Process Pass, Process Fail, Process Fault, when lamp should blink? All query should be converted to form asking options, text data and selection list. Think yourself as Industrial Automation Designer with years of experience in electrical design of special purpose machines . All these crucial details must be collected to understand the customer requirement. Present it in a neat form which can be duly filled during meeting with customer . In top of the page - Name of the Project, Project Number,Customer Name, Date, Site should be given to be filled Thought for 52s PUSH BUTTONS, INDICATORS AND TOWER LAMPS REQUIREMENT FORM (ready for customer meetings) I have created a professional, fillable Excel form based on the provided template sheet, expanded with best practices from years of experience in industrial automation and special purpose machine (SPM) electrical design. Key Enhancements as an Industrial Automation Designer: Header section for project metadata (Project Name, Number, Customer, Date, Site) – essential for documentation and traceability. Structured sections for Push Buttons (Operator location, HMI Panel, E-Stop, Reset) with fields for quantity, purpose, colors, locations. Indicators section (HMI + Operator side). Comprehensive Tower Lamps section with specific states (Machine Idle, Running, Process Pass/Fail, Fault) including color selection, blinking logic, and comments. This captures critical visual status signaling for operators. All fields support text input, numbers, and notes. Use dropdowns (you can easily add them in Excel via Data Validation) for common colors (e.g., Green=Start/OK, Red=Stop/Fault, Yellow=Warning, Blue=Special/Reset, White/Amber=Idle). Tables with borders for neat printing/filling during meetings. Expandable rows for multiple buttons/indicators. Typical Best Practices & Questions to Ask Customer (for your reference during meetings): Colors (IEC 60204 / common standards): Green (Run/Start/OK), Red (Stop/Emergency/Fault), Yellow/Amber (Warning/Standby), Blue (Reset/Info), White (Power On/Idle). Tower Lamp Blinking: Usually for faults or warnings (e.g., blink on Process Fault or Pass/Fail to draw attention). Illuminated Push Buttons: Momentary or latching? With/without legend plates? E-Stop & Reset: Category 0/1 stop? Locations for safety (accessible, visible). HMI: Touch panel size (e.g., 7", 10", 15"), integration with PLC. IP Rating / Environment: Panel vs. field devices (dust, moisture, vibration). Quantity & Redundancy: Based on machine zones, operator stations. Open the attached Excel file in Microsoft Excel or LibreOffice. It is ready to print or fill digitally. You can add data validation lists for colors if needed. If you want a Word version (.docx), more sections (e.g., wiring details, PLC I/O mapping), checkboxes, or customizations, let me know! This ensures all crucial customer requirements are captured systematically for accurate electrical design and quoting. create a html blog keeping the above points where i can add excel sheet lin which can be downloaded by user. Thought for 54s ✅ HTML Blog Page Created Successfully! Here's a clean, professional, ready-to-use single-file HTML blog/post that incorporates all the key points from our previous discussion. How to use it: Copy the code below (or download the file I created). Save it as push-buttons-lamps-requirement.html Open it in any browser — it works offline. You can host it on GitHub Pages, your company website, or share the file directly with customers. Push Buttons, Indicators & Tower Lamps Requirement Form | Industrial Automation

Push Buttons, Indicators & Tower Lamps Requirement Form

For Special Purpose Machines (SPM) - Industrial Automation Projects

Project Information

1. Push Buttons

Best Practices & Standard Color Recommendations (IEC 60204-1)

  • Green: Start / Running / Process OK
  • Red: Stop / Emergency / Fault
  • Yellow / Amber: Warning / Standby
  • Blue: Reset / Information
  • White: Power ON / Idle
